methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Sublime Systems is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Leah Ellis and Yet-Ming Chiang that has raised over $200M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same-stage private companies including Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, CarbonCapture Inc., and Cemvita, alongside one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $200M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B progression, yielding $380M. The peer-median method examines five Climate & Energy peers at Series B stage, with valuations ranging from $120M (Cemvita) to $500M (Pano AI), producing a median implied valuation of $310M. These two methods generate a broad implied range before averaging.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation corridor of $264M (low) to $437M (high), with a median point of $345M. The width of this range reflects material dispersion in the peer set and the methodological gap between stage-progression and peer comparables. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the five-peer sample for Sublime's specific technology and market position, and whether stage-progression multiples—derived from macro fundraising patterns—appropriately capture this target's trajectory within an emerging subsector.
